title: Kamna Devi Temple
tagline: Kamna Devi Temple — spiritual town of Himachal Pradesh

overview.short: Kamna Devi Temple is a Hindu temple located in Boileauganj, Shimla, Himachal Pradesh.
overview.description: Kamna Devi Temple is a Hindu temple located in Boileauganj, Shimla, Himachal Pradesh. It is situated atop of Prospect Hill which is one of the seven hills in Shimla. The temple is situated on steep road to hilltop above Boileauganj.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Totu, Shimla
topPlaces.description: Totu is a part of Shimla city, in the state of Himachal Pradesh, India. It is a populous urban area of the city.

topPlaces.name: Boileauganj, Shimla
topPlaces.description: Boileauganj is a part of Shimla in the North Indian state of Himachal Pradesh. Boileauganj is one of the old and historically important localities of Shimla.

topPlaces.name: Rashtrapati Niwas
topPlaces.description: The Rashtrapati Niwas (literally the "President's Residence"), formerly known as Viceregal Lodge, is located on the Observatory Hill in Boileauganj, Shimla, Himachal Pradesh, India. It was formerly the residence of the British Viceroy of India.

topPlaces.name: Seven Hills of Shimla
topPlaces.description: Seven Hills of Shimla are the seven hills in Shimla, the capital and the largest city of the North Indian state of Himachal Pradesh. It served as the summer capital of British India.

topPlaces.name: Jutogh
topPlaces.description: Jutogh is a part of Shimla and it is a cantonment board, in the North Indian state of Himachal Pradesh. The Jutogh cantonment was established in 1843 Cantonment Board.

topPlaces.name: Sankat Mochan Temple, Shimla
topPlaces.description: Sankat Mochan Temple is a Hindu temple, dedicated to Hindu deity Hanuman in Shimla, Himachal Pradesh, India. The temple is Shimla's second most visited Hanuman temple after Jakhu Temple.

topPlaces.name: Himachal State Museum
topPlaces.description: The Himachal State Museum is a cultural museum located in Shimla, in the Indian Himalayan state of Himachal Pradesh. The museum was established in 1974 in Inverarm, a house at the top of Inverarm Hill that during the British Raj was the hot season residence of Lord William Beresford (and frequently used for overflow guests at the Viceroy's house in Shimla, Peterhoff, which was smaller), and subsequently of Edwin Henry Hayter Collen and then Edward FitzGerald Law.

topPlaces.name: Tutikandi
topPlaces.description: Tutikandi is a part of Shimla city, Himachal Pradesh, India. It has the main Inter State Bus Terminal of Shimla.

items.note: Totu is a part of Shimla city, in the state of Himachal Pradesh, India. It is a populous urban area of the city.
Boileauganj is a part of Shimla in the North Indian state of Himachal Pradesh. Boileauganj is one of the old and historically important localities of Shimla.

The Rashtrapati Niwas (literally the "President's Residence"), formerly known as Viceregal Lodge, is located on the Observatory Hill in Boileauganj, Shimla, Himachal Pradesh, India. It was formerly the residence of the British Viceroy of India.

items.note: Seven Hills of Shimla are the seven hills in Shimla, the capital and the largest city of the North Indian state of Himachal Pradesh. It served as the summer capital of British India.
Jutogh is a part of Shimla and it is a cantonment board, in the North Indian state of Himachal Pradesh. The Jutogh cantonment was established in 1843 Cantonment Board.
Sankat Mochan Temple is a Hindu temple, dedicated to Hindu deity Hanuman in Shimla, Himachal Pradesh, India. The temple is Shimla's second most visited Hanuman temple after Jakhu Temple.

The Himachal State Museum is a cultural museum located in Shimla, in the Indian Himalayan state of Himachal Pradesh. The museum was established in 1974 in Inverarm, a house at the top of Inverarm Hill that during the British Raj was the hot season residence of Lord William Beresford (and frequently used for overflow guests at the Viceroy's house in Shimla, Peterhoff, which was smaller), and subsequently of Edwin Henry Hayter Collen and then Edward FitzGerald Law.
Tutikandi is a part of Shimla city, Himachal Pradesh, India. It has the main Inter State Bus Terminal of Shimla.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kamna Devi Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kamna Devi Temple is Mar – Jun, Sep – Nov. Summers are 15–28°C and winters are 2–16°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Kamna Devi Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Shimla Airport (Jubbarhatti) (6 km) and the nearest railway station is Chandigarh Railway Station (55 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kamna Devi Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kamna Devi Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kamna Devi Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Kamna Devi Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kamna Devi Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Totu, Shimla, Boileauganj, Shimla, Rashtrapati Niwas, Seven Hills of Shimla, Jutogh.
